name: always_returns_error
code:
proof fn phi_1_always_returns_error(old_os: Os, new_os: Os, thread_ptr: ThreadPtr, endpoint_index: EndpointIdx, ret: SyscallReturnStruct)
    requires
        old_os.wf(),
        old_os.thread_dom().contains(thread_ptr),
        0 <= endpoint_index < MAX_NUM_ENDPOINT_DESCRIPTORS,
        new_os.wf(),
        syscall_new_thread_with_endpoint_spec(old_os, new_os, thread_ptr, endpoint_index, ret),
    ensures
        ret == SyscallReturnStruct::NoSwitchNew(RetValueType::Error),
{
}
reason: If provable, the spec is vacuous—thread creation can never succeed regardless of system state.

name: success_returns_same_thread_ptr
code:
proof fn phi_2_success_returns_same_thread_ptr(old_os: Os, new_os: Os, thread_ptr: ThreadPtr, endpoint_index: EndpointIdx, ret: SyscallReturnStruct)
    requires
        old_os.wf(),
        old_os.thread_dom().contains(thread_ptr),
        0 <= endpoint_index < MAX_NUM_ENDPOINT_DESCRIPTORS,
        new_os.wf(),
        syscall_new_thread_with_endpoint_spec(old_os, new_os, thread_ptr, endpoint_index, ret),
        ret != SyscallReturnStruct::NoSwitchNew(RetValueType::Error),
    ensures
        ret == SyscallReturnStruct::NoSwitchNew(RetValueType::SuccessUsize { value: thread_ptr }),
{
}
reason: If provable, the "new" thread pointer aliases the calling thread, violating thread identity uniqueness.

name: error_changes_thread_dom
code:
proof fn phi_3_error_changes_thread_dom(old_os: Os, new_os: Os, thread_ptr: ThreadPtr, endpoint_index: EndpointIdx, ret: SyscallReturnStruct)
    requires
        old_os.wf(),
        old_os.thread_dom().contains(thread_ptr),
        0 <= endpoint_index < MAX_NUM_ENDPOINT_DESCRIPTORS,
        new_os.wf(),
        syscall_new_thread_with_endpoint_spec(old_os, new_os, thread_ptr, endpoint_index, ret),
        ret == SyscallReturnStruct::NoSwitchNew(RetValueType::Error),
    ensures
        old_os.thread_dom() !== new_os.thread_dom(),
{
}
reason: If provable, error paths have side effects on the thread domain, violating rollback semantics.

name: success_thread_dom_unchanged
code:
proof fn phi_4_success_thread_dom_unchanged(old_os: Os, new_os: Os, thread_ptr: ThreadPtr, endpoint_index: EndpointIdx, ret: SyscallReturnStruct)
    requires
        old_os.wf(),
        old_os.thread_dom().contains(thread_ptr),
        0 <= endpoint_index < MAX_NUM_ENDPOINT_DESCRIPTORS,
        new_os.wf(),
        syscall_new_thread_with_endpoint_spec(old_os, new_os, thread_ptr, endpoint_index, ret),
        ret != SyscallReturnStruct::NoSwitchNew(RetValueType::Error),
    ensures
        old_os.thread_dom() =~= new_os.thread_dom(),
{
}
reason: If provable, a successful thread creation adds no new thread to the domain, making the operation a no-op.

name: container_pages_modified
code:
proof fn phi_5_container_pages_modified(old_os: Os, new_os: Os, thread_ptr: ThreadPtr, endpoint_index: EndpointIdx, ret: SyscallReturnStruct, c: ContainerPtr)
    requires
        old_os.wf(),
        old_os.thread_dom().contains(thread_ptr),
        0 <= endpoint_index < MAX_NUM_ENDPOINT_DESCRIPTORS,
        new_os.wf(),
        syscall_new_thread_with_endpoint_spec(old_os, new_os, thread_ptr, endpoint_index, ret),
        new_os.container_dom().contains(c),
    ensures
        old_os.get_container_owned_pages(c) !== new_os.get_container_owned_pages(c),
{
}
reason: If provable, the spec permits container page ownership to change, violating the explicit preservation invariant asserted in the implementation.
